summary: Targeted regression of the two iteration-10 fixes — both VERIFIED via the public preview URL. Backend: POST /api/business/ideas/seed-1/plan returned HTTP 200 in 50.2s (was timing out at 60s Cloudflare proxy in iter 10) with a 6.4KB markdown plan containing the required ## sections; POST /api/shopping/utilities/ga_power/find-better returned HTTP 200 in 21.3s with a 2.4KB recommendation. Both now comfortably fit within the ~60s Cloudflare window. Frontend: Logged in as test1@plos.app on the web preview at 390x844 viewport. Active Deals → tapped dismiss-d2; window.confirm fired ('Dismiss this deal? You won’t see it again…'), auto-accepted, deals count 4 → 3, savings card recalculated to $66. Registered Products → opened add modal, registered 'TestProd', then tapped first delete trash; window.confirm fired ('Remove product? You won’t receive recall alerts…'), auto-accepted, products count 2 → 1. Both flows now actually mutate state on web, confirming the Platform.OS === 'web' window.confirm branch works. No new regressions observed.
action_items: (Carry-over, not blocking) Consider splitting /app/backend/server.py (~4737 lines) into per-module routers and moving SEED_BUSINESS_IDEAS / DEFAULT_EDEN_HEIGHTS / SEED_DEALS / SEED_UTILITIES into /app/backend/seed/*.py — flagged in iter 10, still pending.

(Optional) The plan endpoint still rides close to the 60s ceiling (50.2s observed). If Claude latency drifts upward or the prompt is enriched, consider an async-job/polling pattern as a permanent safety net.

(Cosmetic) Console warnings: 'shadow* style props are deprecated. Use boxShadow' and 'props.pointerEvents is deprecated. Use style.pointerEvents' — react-native-web deprecations from a dependency; not blocking.
critical_code_review_comments: Fix 1 implementation (server.py:4159-4168 + 4250): prompt now explicitly bounds Claude to ~1500 words for plan and ~500 words for find-better. Clean and minimal change — verified to bring both endpoints under the 60s Cloudflare ceiling.

Fix 2 implementation (deals.tsx:71-90, products.tsx:61-78): Platform.OS === 'web' branch with window.confirm is correctly gated and only runs on web, preserving native Alert.alert behaviour for iOS/Android. Verified end-to-end on the web preview.
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/test_iteration_11_fixes.py (NEW — 2 focused regression tests for the Cloudflare-timeout fix)

success_rate.backend: 100% (2/2 targeted regression tests passed within the 60s Cloudflare window)
success_rate.frontend: 100% (both Alert.alert → window.confirm web flows verified: deals dismiss 4→3 and product delete 2→1, with confirm() messages captured)

seed_data_creation: No new seed data. Test re-used the seeded business idea seed-1, seeded utility ga_power, seeded deals (d1..d5 minus d1 already dismissed from prior runs leaving 4 active), and the registered_products collection for test1@plos.app — added TestProd then removed it during the run.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iter 10's two blockers are both resolved. (1) Backend Claude endpoints /api/business/ideas/{id}/plan and /api/shopping/utilities/{id}/find-better now respond within 60s via the public URL (50.2s and 21.3s observed). (2) shopping/deals.tsx dismiss() and shopping/products.tsx removeItem() now use a Platform.OS web branch with window.confirm and work on the web preview. For future Playwright runs on this app, override window.confirm via page.add_init_script before any navigation — Playwright's built-in dialog event handler races with the click action and causes click() to time out; the JS override (window.confirm = () => true with a __confirms log) is the reliable pattern. Login testIDs are login-email-input / login-password-input / login-submit-button (not login-email/login-submit).
rca_of_issue: N/A for this iteration — both fixes verified working. Iter 10 root causes were: (a) Claude was producing ~33KB markdown plans taking ~235s end-to-end which exceeded Cloudflare's ~60s proxy timeout — fixed by tightening the prompt to <1500 words with explicit section budgets, bringing total round-trip to ~50s; (b) React Native's Alert.alert button onPress callbacks don't fire on react-native-web (it falls back to window.alert) — fixed by adding a Platform.OS === 'web' branch that uses window.confirm() directly before invoking the same performDismiss/performRemove async path.
